Camera Setup

Camera off

Camera Tramming

Align your camera so the bed edges are parallel to the grid lines. Use the crosshair to centre the camera over the bed.

Adjust your camera mount so the bed edges align with the grid lines and the crosshair is centred on the bed.

ChArUco Board Generation

Generate a printable ChArUco board for camera calibration. A larger board gives more visible corners and better coverage, but keep the print flat, sharp, and printed at 100% scale. For intrinsics, an A4 or A3 board is often easier to move around the bed and tilt through different poses.

Camera Calibration (Intrinsics)

Intrinsics calibration measures the camera lens model: focal length, optical center, and distortion. Capture at least 6 usable frames; 10-12 is a better target, and 15 is worthwhile for wide or inexpensive lenses. Keep the same resolution, focus, zoom, and camera mount after calibration.
Use varied board poses: center, edges, corners, rotated, and slightly tilted toward/away from the camera. A full-bed ChArUco board is useful for coverage and later bed homography, but still mix in A4/A3 shots around the edges and corners if you can move them. Flat, centered repeats are weak calibration data.
0 frames
Mean fit—
Max frame—
Bed scaleDefine bed area
Frame Board Corners Error Quality Flags
No calibration frames captured yet.

Bed Calibration (Homography)

Place the ChArUco board flat on the CNC bed.
